objective: Address code-quality boundaries identified in the review. Separate updater models and GitHub transport, centralize game-name sanitization, place updater state under PluginUpdater, centralize Steam runtime validation, decompose large frontend modules (gameLifecycleController, autoSyncStatusSurface), split the large Syncthing test suite, and add architecture module budgets.
design_decisions: Updater models were separated into a pure dataclass file to eliminate cyclic imports and untyped dictionaries.
Updater client was separated to strictly isolate all urllib.request transport.
PluginUpdater was given sole ownership of updater operations, state, cache persistence, and installer reconciliation.
SDHLudusaviService delegates all updater RPC calls to PluginUpdater via the facade pattern.
Game-name sanitization was centralized to a single `sanitize_game_name` implementation.
gameLifecycleController was split, introducing `steamLifecycleSource` to decouple origin events from business logic.
autoSyncStatusSurface was heavily factored down by separating HTML rendering to `autoSyncStatusRenderer.tsx` and CEF view native bounds to `autoSyncStatusBrowserView.ts`.

Monolithic frontend static pattern matching assertions were converted to more robust architectural validations via python regexes in test_architectural_constraints.py and text size line limits in test_module_size_budgets.py.
results: All architectural boundaries were successfully established without regressions in functional coverage, UI layout, or user-facing behavior. Unit tests run completely green.
validation: Locally executed: ruff format, ruff check, ty check, pytest, pnpm run verify, pnpm run typecheck, pnpm run build.
review_resolutions: Code review execution and integration pending review gate.
